reference: I am sorry to hear you are feeling so bad, as neck pain on top of a cold can be quite demoralizing. The dizziness is most probably a result of the cold you have, and steam inhalation three times a day should help you. If it doesn't, I suggest a physician review where I am sure medications like Sturgeon or Betahistine would be prescribed. The neck pain seems more like a whiplash, and for now, bed rest and a mild painkiller like Paracetamol should do the trick. A cold compress on the neck would help too. If things don't get better in a couple of days, physical therapy may be needed. I hope you feel better soon.
text: I am sorry to hear you are feeling so bad. As neck pain on top of a cold can be quite demoralizing. The dizziness is most probably a result of the cold you have, and steam inhalation three times a day should help you. If it doesn't, I suggest a physician review, where I am sure medications like Sturgeon or Betasistine would be prescribed. The neck pain seems more like a whiplash, and for now, bed rest and a mild painkiller like Paracetamol should do the trick. A cold compress on the neck would help too. If things don't get better in a couple of days, physical therapy may be needed. I hope you feel better soon.
